body_txt: The Blank Stare is usually performed by one character in response to another's actions or verbal tirade, either in mind-numbing disbelief or disappointing awe, and especially when the latter character happens to go off the rails or otherwise show an aspect of themselves wildly different from what was expected. It is usually performed primarily in a visual medium such as film, television, or graphic novels, and is done for both humorous and dramatic effect; the dramatic type tends to display the moment when you can no longer depend on that particular character anymore, while the comedic one usually reveals a WTF-type scenario similar to a Double Take or a Spit Take . The verbal version of this, which usually accompanies one of these, is the Flat "What" . Compare Thousand-Yard Stare , which is similar, but is meant to showcase the extreme trauma the stare-giver has gone through. Also see Stunned Silence .
